ShortDescription.Value: Quickly toggle mouse edge scrolling on or off with a hotkey or an on-screen button.

Publish.LongDescription:
Quickly turn the camera's mouse edge scrolling on or off - no more digging through the options menu every time.
## Use Case If you're running a multi-monitor setup or with a small screen, you may find that the mouse edge scrolling is sometimes annoying. Even more annoying is when you alt-tab to check your email or order pizza and come back to find the camera has moved to the edge of the map.
## Features - Inspired by algernon's [Toggle Edge Scrolling mod](https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2645254941) for Cities: Skylines. - Toggle edge scrolling with a configurable hotkey (default Ctrl + Shift + E). - Optional on-screen toggle button, shown either in the main UI (top-right of the screen) or in the game's mod button menu. - The button is green when edge scrolling is on and red when it is off. - Optionally disable edge scrolling automatically on startup. - Optionally disable edge scrolling while the game window is in the background, then restore it when the window is focused again.
## Optional - Unified Icon Library - if installed, the toggle button uses a matching game-style icon. It is not required; without it the mod uses its own bundled icon.
## Usage Configure the hotkey and button placement under Options > Toggle Edge Scrolling.
## Notes - HUGE thank you to all of the actual mod makers out there who have made this game so much more fun! Thank you also for sharing your knowledge and examples.
